evals.prompt: My collision-engineers-dvsa-mot extension is installed in Claude Desktop and I can see it listed under extensions, but when I open a chat there are no tools at all. The plus icon doesn't even show up. I haven't changed anything.
evals.expected_output: Should detect MSIX vs Standard install, check mcp.log for config or load errors, validate claude_desktop_config.json JSON, check for the dxt-install directory and whether the extension's server log exists, and provide specific PowerShell commands the user can run.

evals.prompt: Claude Desktop just shows a completely white screen when I open it. I tried reinstalling it twice and it still won't load. Windows 11, brand new laptop.
evals.expected_output: Should suggest --disable-gpu-compositing launch flag first, then --disable-gpu as fallback, explain how to add it to the shortcut, and point to Event Viewer for deeper diagnosis.

evals.prompt: Every time Windows boots, Claude Desktop crashes straight away. If I wait a minute and open it from the start menu it works perfectly fine. It's really annoying.
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as the autostart DLL crash pattern, instruct user to disable autostart via Task Manager Startup Apps, and suggest the shell:startup shortcut workaround.

evals.prompt: All my MCP tools disappeared after I updated Claude Desktop last week. I can see the servers listed in the config file but nothing shows in the app.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the MSIX config path mismatch as the primary hypothesis (tools worked before update, stopped after), provide the Get-AppxPackage detection command, explain the two-path issue, and show how to find the real active config path.

evals.prompt: Claude Desktop keeps logging me out. Every time I open it I have to sign in again with Google. It's been happening for about three days.
evals.expected_output: Should check for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable conflict first (most non-obvious cause), then guide through clearing the OAuth token cache (config.json), and explain the sign-out/sign-in flow.
